response: The rock band that sang "All Join Hands," which is by Slade, rose to prominence in the early 1970s. Slade, an English glam rock band from Wolverhampton, achieved significant success during this period with 17 consecutive top 20 hits and six number ones on the UK Singles Chart. They were recognized as the most successful British group of the 1970s based on sales of singles and are noted for having three singles enter the charts at number one, with all six of their chart-toppers penned by Noddy Holder and Jim Lea.
